text: T-mobile 5G is the worst. They claim to be better than everyone else but they are not. 5G does not work everywhere and it slows down even if you have full bars. I would rather have cricket than T-mobile.

T-Mobile by far is the worst customer service I have experienced. When they bought out Sprint they left us Sprint customers without service and lied to us when we purchased a new phone that we would continue to receive service in our home like we did with sprint. Now we have a phone but no T mobile service. The representative are uninformed of the merger issues and phone issues that occur as a result of this  the transfer.  Their are a multitude of issues.  Get out while you can !

I give T-Mobile a one rating because I can go no lower.  Worst service I've ever had.  Technical personnel verified power is not even important my direction.  If you want a carrier with no signal T-Mobile is the one.

T-Mobile's customer service team is not qualified and knowledgeable. I was on the phone with them for 25-30 trying to explain the situation that my mobile hotspot is not working properly, the service sucks. I was not able to stream and surf the web. The young lady stated, “your device is working properly”. My plan has restarted, so there is no way I should have issues with the plan and or service. Customer Service is not great listeners or troubleshooters. I politely disconnected the call and will terminate my services, smh.

I switched from Verizon to T-Mobile. BIG mistake. I upgraded to a newer I-Phone and everyone said it sounded like I was talking through a tin can. I called several times with no help from T-Mobile. I fulfilled my agreement with T-Mobile and immediately switched back to Verizon. Three months after disconnecting my account I received a collection letter from T-Mobile saying I owed them over $200.00. This is the worst company ever. I have called BBB and all my credit agencies to dispute their claim. 
BEWARE 

To discuss
I get 0.5 kb on 5g and 1mb on 4g in this zip code area ( 91335 )

I just got off the phone with T-Mobile one of those animated things I thought it was just a robot but no it really was a person so they got some great deal going on get a free phone sign up with them but the thing is I asked him if they could beat what I pay right now per month and they said it depends on my credit well I told him I don't have credit I've never done credit so how are they going to gauge good credit bad credit on my phone bill that's almost discriminating. I wanted to pay them cash straight up for the phone and start a new service believe it or not I couldn't I don't know what I gather is if you got bad credit don't bother going there everyone have a great 4th of July
Caliy Kidd

If I had the opportunity to do this over, I wouldn’t. Several weeks how many phone calls later, I have still not been able to apply for the rebate you were promised when you transferred to T-Mobile with a device that has an outstanding balance with another carrier. The paperwork/process make it almost impossible to qualify for the rebate. In addition, the store/salesman I was dealing with we’re closed for over a week. The service hasn’t been bad, but it’s been impossible to complete the transfer
Horrible technical support. 5 ways verification and then no patience for anything else. Nothing is done. The phone doesn’t work.
Do NOT get T-Mobile. Their cellphone service and customer service is more than lousy!! Save yourself the aggravation and frustration.
The connection is bad while driving, so the music is often cut off.  I have never experienced it with at&t. Slow and very disappointing.
Terrible service with worse customer service.  If you want to be able to use your service  do not go with T mobile.  Couldn't  be more dissatisfied.

My experience  today at the Grand Junction, Co store was Amazing!.Brenda went above and beyond  taking  care of me..Her and Angelica  were Outstanding.Very friendly..informative..and very professional.It was a rating of a 10 experience..Thank you!

Have had no problem with phone service but internet service is is now on par with Centurylink, which has the worst service in the world. When first starting with t-mobile internet when problems occurred could talk to someone in the U.S. but now calling tech service you get someone in the Phillipines who can barely speak English and have no clue to what they are doing.

My fiance lost her phone in the river while in Pennsylvania.  We went to the West Goshen Shopping Center and Kristofer couldn't be more helpful.  He helped us get the best deal on the phone we purchased.  He took the time to switch out both our SIM cards and even helped my fiance pick/find a password for her google account until she got all her contacts back.  If I had to get a new phone I would go back to him n Westchester PA even though I live in NY.  He couldn't be friendlier and more helpful!  a+
Don't do business with T-Mobile they do not stand behind a word

I bought a 5G Samsung phone from T-mobile. I couldn't make phone calls more than 85% of the time. Messages can't be delivered either. The store only wants me to exchange it because it's passed its 14 days policy. I have spent about 2 hours every time when I went back to the store to take care of this problem. They want me to go to Samsung directly to get it taken care of. I now have exchanged the phone, but it's giving me the same problem, and they want me to exchange it again because it's within the warranty from Samsung. T-mobile is not taking any responsibility. I just spent 40minutes on the phone with the supervisor who told me all she can do is to exchange the same phone again for me.

Worst service in America. Possibly the World. Unlimited lie!!!

Everyday at 4:30 PM, no internet. Throttled. Can't do anything. 11:00 PM, almost back to normal. Midnight, I can use the internet.

I do not get more than two bars at any given time.

Calling them only makes it worse.

Do not sign up for this. They truly suck balls.
Since sprint sold out to T-Mobile the service for at home internet has been terrible. Won’t hardly work using computer or on my cell phone. I miss Sprint.
Worst cellular company I have ever had. One of the worst consumer decisions I have ever made.

Worst data service - I live just three miles from one of the busiest interstates in the Northeast, and I have ZERO data at my home.  If I am not on the top floor of my home, the incoming calls don't get through. Its a joke. I truly suspect I'm getting data throttling by T-Mobile.

Continue to charge me for things that were supposed to be taken off my bill after an upgrade unwilling to remove charges lady got an attitude with my wife when she refused to listen to our side of the story . Put us on hold multiple times did not get her name and she refused to give her name when asked before being hung up on. I was a loyal sprint customer for 10 years since tmobile has taken over customer service has dwindled down to nothing . Never had these problems with sprint

Switched over to another company due to coverage issues while my husband works (truck driver). Always had good experiences until we switched over. 

Apparently it's company policy that if you take your number with you, you still pay for the entire month of service even if you move over on the 2nd day of your billing cycle. 

They also will not take payment on your final bill until you receive the bill in the mail. You receive a collections notice in the mail before you even receive the final bill (moved service over on 5/2, collections letter dated 5/14 and never late once on a monthy bill, final bill dated 5/20)

Due to these poor "policies" stay far, far away.

They will actively make your life hell if you try to leave the company. I switched to verizon over 2 months ago and I'm still dealing with the aftermath of closing down my tmobile account. I would give less than one star if I could.
Worst thing they do is purposely fail to let you know that you Must return the "cellspot" within 30 days. You'll wake up to a threatening letter charging you hundreds for a device you've completely forgotten about. I scrambled to get a return label and get it back to them as soon as possible, but they failed three separate times at sending it. They told me I'd receive it in 30 minutes and hung up. Guess what? It absolutely did not come in 30 minutes. Apparently sending a simple email with a return label is far too difficult of a process for their completely inept customer service, but sending a collector to harass me and my family for the $300 bill they sent me comes pretty easily to them. Taking it directly to the store is also useless, as their 25 year old tech-savvy employees are somehow clueless about the process of returning the damned box. I've currently been on the phone with a fourth customer service representative from god knows where for over two hours, she keeps calling out to people who are laughing in the background in another language, only to come back and tell me to do what I've already done a million times. They are by far the slowest, most untrained, underqualified, and unprofessional customer service I have ever dealt with. It has been an infuriating process that I would not recommend to anyone. I think my hair starting falling out from the stress it caused me. Just go with verizon or at&t, it's not worth it.

We were Sprint customers for over 15 years.  5/22: Went to store to get husband new phone.  Told about merger with T-Mobile.  Better service, more coverage bc have both Sprint/T Mobile towers, and have a better plan - lower cost.   I specifically asked about coverage and was told would be the same. Looked at some phones, guy advised one and said if bought outright would be more expensive than installment plan. So, went with installment plan.  5/26:  Called T Mobile: Phones not receiving calls and can't call out. 2.18 HOURS later and 4 people, solution was to switch back to Sprint.  5/28: T Mobile advised can't provide the service, cant go back to Sprint, go to another carrier. Must pay off phone before TMobile would unlock. Paid off via credit card over the phone.  5/29: Learned from Verizon that new cell phone only compatible with TMobile (no one at the store, Phone Rep, or Tech rep told us that).  5/29: Store states they can provide TRADE IN VALUE less $45 restocking fee.  6/1:  TMobile:  Explained situation.  Response:  IF A DEVICE IS PAID IN FULL ORIGINALLY DONE ON INSTALLMENT PLAN (even if that was the 1 and only payment), CANNOT RETURN PHONE FOR A REFUND AND BUYER'S REMORSE DOES NOT APPLY.  The Customer Rep, "I apologize for the misinformation."  Contract states:  Return policy.  You can return or exchange a device for a refund within 14 days of the purchase date of the original device.  NO where does it state, EXCEPT on an installment plan that you paid via phone at the advice of the company rep = you cannot return or get a refund.
T-mobile has horrible internet,don't even get me started on the the customer service.Everyone was rude and disrespectful.Over all T-mobile does not deserve customers or the recognition they have.

T-Mobile has the worst sales and service I have ever experienced. The sales quote on the phone was less of course than the bill we received. After speaking to the first called customer service person she put me on hold for about a minute came back put me on hold for about eight minutes. She was the one I canceled my service with. Which she didn’t cancel or indicate on any records. She just got mad because she couldn’t receive commission and hung up on me. I have only had for chips for 12 days and never installed them. Which they have a 14 day trial/cancellation period. Seven phone calls and four hours later they say I owe them for two months $160. Of course they threaten to put me in collections. There is nobody at T-Mobile I can help. Don’t get caught up with them like I did.

They don’t even deserve 1 star! They do nothing but lie! They have the worst coverage of anyone even metro has better coverage then T-Mobile yes I know same company but it’s true! I talked to several customer service reps(who don’t speak English very well) and they all lie say they will call back and don’t! I even went into the store to resolve my issue and that got me nowhere! Here I am not even a month later with a $800 bill for the phones they wouldn’t send me a return label for! They told me on the phone I had 30 days to return the phones and I switched my service after like 22 days and they then said I only have 14 days! What a bunch of liars! They are the worst company I have ever dealt with save yourself some headache STAY AWAY FROM T-MOBILE!!!!
Horrible at best

I have called Tmobile about my connectivity issues and it is terrible. I have a 5G phone but the connection is so slow. Even with my high speed internet. I would not recommend them for anyone. The customer service is good but they need to update their system!
The t mobile Tuesday app is a total scam that won’t even load the page correctly on your phone screen for you to complete the game/prizes the correct way. SCAM.

What is the deal with their web site?!  Crazy slow, takes MINUTES to get to the login page, enter credentials, then it errors out.  The T-Mobile site has been pretty bad for years, but now it is basically 100% useless.  One would think they would at least be able to authenticate users who are trying to PAY them money.  Whoever is running their web services needs to be fired.

I wish I could give them zero stars. When a company makes promises then changes the plan it is immoral and I believe illegal. When I signed up with T-Mobile my plan had unlimited data and unlimited 4G lte hot spot. Since they bought out Sprint they have been throttling the HS at >50GB. When I spoke to a representative they agreed that they changed the terms of my plan (but continue to charge me for it) and there is nothing I can do about it. I'm thinking this is a class action lawsuit in the making. Soon I will be canceling T-Mobile. I have contacted fairshake.com because corporations should not be able to screw their customers for profit.

While I had been a customer of sprint for 6 years, I had never had a problem with getting an upgrade. Mind you I had 5 lines on my plan. I spent well over 5 hours trying to figure why I could not upgrade and why they would ask for nearly the cost of the phone to upgrade… it’s been a few weeks now and I have yet to get an answer. After being told I would get a call back, and they would fix the issue… I’m still waiting for that call. 

I actually decided to change carriers, and am now with Verizon. My bill was cut in half and I had to pay zero to get my new phones. Too bad T-mobile took over, I’ve not had a bad experience until this merger! ✌🏽

I walked into a Tmobile store and I was offered a promotional deal which I took.  Now I'm being charged for that. I went back to the store and was told the agent made a mistake and I'm responsible for her mistake. I've been with Tmobile for over 5 years and this is how they treat me.

I wouldn’t even leave one star they would let me leave a review without one I would give it zero nobody go to them sprint was great before they merged with T-Mobile I did the mistake of getting a contract when it was still sprint before it merged when emerge happened everything went to hell when I first came to sprint your typical going to a new phone company issues little bugs to work out no big deal next thing you know they merged with Sprint everything goes to hell they start messing with my account my bill raised $100 for no reason I was abused by customer service and so was my boyfriend who is on voice because he is on the account to talk to you also, they caught the representative trying to erase the recording he was tired because they heard the abuse on the line they finally fix the bill and the contract later they said that I had a lease on phones which they were all paid off on all four I owned them all then none of our phones from the very beginning ever works properly we even took him into the store and even worked on them through the phone they can’t figure out what’s wrong with them and they will not replace them you have to wait on hold and you get transferred to people over and over getting having to repeat everything you can’t talk to anybody officially they don’t know their business. The broken change my passcode everything else then change them back I locked me out of my account. After the first time jacking with my contract subject with it again and I was waiting on a call from the CEO again they were knowing that I was going to have surgery they waited till my surgery date and they knew the date and I wouldn’t be able to talk and they called didn’t bother to call me back I called back after waiting for them to call me back took forever to get a hold of somebody took over day promise to see you will call me back I waited a week by that Friday nobody called I called back all day Friday on the phone till 11 PM at night promised within 48 hours CEO been escalated again I don’t know how many times case been escalated see the crimes of been committed by their company towards me and also they knew I was keeping copies of the chat and I had witnesses. Kept lying to me and turning things still keep trying to chat with me even till I told them that my health and because I’ve been on the phone is aggravated by stressors and I’m in extreme pain because I just had surgery on my jaw not even a month ago it’ll take over a year to heal they’ve had me sit in the store with my whole family that’s disabled have an appointment with me to try to work on our phones we got to the store on our appointment time but in the store The manager said they didn’t even have us down for an appointment I showed him my email to confirm my appointment time at that exact store she had no reasoning we spent five hours and I have no immune system and they know it. And it wasn’t just me it was my whole family with health problems and they still can’t figure out with her phones so 90% of the time we had our phones and they extended my lease it’s already up I wanna raise a grandfather clause but now they’re saying no I still and I owe my phones again how can you pay off your phones and all again when you tell them to quit chatting with you cause you’re not getting anywhere and you’re waiting for the phone call back that you were expecting they won’t leave you alone even when you tell them that you’re in so much pain you can’t stand it you’re about ready to go to the emergency room. They promised they’re going to resolve and fix your problems, to get disconnected and hook to somebody else for you have to repeat everything and it’s going to be escalated and go to the same thing I have been on here for five hours and talk to over six people non-stop without a break and they want to know what this time I can help you know they can never help you and you never get who you can to help you it used to be you could be directly sent to the CEO or they have the company but since the last time that I got to talk to one about a year ago when they first met with my bill when the merger completed this lady was actually with sprint originally and she took over my account and put my bill back to work but it was originally and I’ve been paying that until this month and they jumped it to almost $60 more for no reason they said I was underneath a special promotion thing which was not in my contract and I never signed up for did they change my passcode my secret security question so they said I couldn’t get information on my stuff then they changed it back and then said it was cracked and I’ve got emails to prove it all but they keep going on and this is been going on for two years and I’m disabled my whole family as we have one income coming in this house my disability check we barely make it we have to have the phones for the doctors and everything they don’t care and we’re not on assistanceWe’re not making it !

I will like to say thank you to Maria(1394002) for outstanding job on assisting me with everything that I called in on dealing my T-Mobile account . She was pleasant and very knowledgeable, and friendly. Thank you for an amazing experience today!

I was a Sprint Business customer for over 20 years with no issues. My Sprint Business contract was up, went to a company owned T- Mobile store to return my Sprint phones and get new phones. I was told they could not port my phone numbers from Sprint and had to call T- Mobile customer service, I spend 2 hours on the phone and was told I had to fill out some online forms and wait 4 days for T-Mobile to call me back to verify. I walked out and went to Verizon, they ported my phone numbers from Sprint. Verizon also gave me a BOGO IPhone 12 Pro Max and $400 MasterCard. Bottom line, T-Mobile hate Sprint customers!

I have had t-mobile for 15 years and have finally had enough. Their service is horrible and their coverage is horrible. They lie and don't seem to care. Put in supports tickes for dropped calls and they told me to disconnect 5g? Then why pay for it? Then they said turn on wifi calling? That's free. Customer service sucks and all they say is sorry and don't do anything. They are not transparent and don't care about customers. I am going to Verizon.  Hopefully they are better. Just unfortunate after 15 years.
Do not get Tmobile…worst service in America!!!

I don't think I even need write how terrible T Mobile is. Everyone has basically written everything I was going to say. Lately our Internet and service has been cutting out off and on for about 2 weeks now. 

For some reason raised our bill on our family plan? We have been having problems with then for awhile. I had Sprint before this and Sprint sucked as well.

 Verizon and ATT have been the only services to me that have been reliable! Pricey but worth it. I definitely recommend to get anybody but T Mobile. They suck and I don't know how they are still in business. Caused a lot of issues! Especially when I was doing my CNA class virtually and the wifi cuts off. DO NOT USE THIS SERVICE.

We went to the West Bellfort T-Mobile branch for my husband's issues with his car device. Mr. Zahi Pena , with his Branch Manager assisted us excellently. They were very accomodating specially Mr. Zahi Pena. He was very polite,helpful and an demonstrated expertise helping us. Lucky for T-Mobile to have Mr. Zahi Pena. We told him if there's a review moret than a ten or 5 stars, we'll gladly give him that.Thank you, Mr. Zahi Pena.

We have been TMobile customers for about 5 years. We live in a rural area and no phone companies service is great here. TMobile is the best we can get it seems. When the service is up and running correctly it is pretty good, not great tho. Calls are dropped, much of the time no bars, we have had multiple problems with different phones. The customer svc is friendly but not very knowledgable. The service techs mostly are hard to understand bec very few speak English. They always tell me the same thing: There is an issue at my tower they are having to fix. Once, they told me they were upgrading our tower. We had little to no service for about 3 months. Then, finally it got better. Stayed better for a few months, then right back to continuous outages, issue with your tower, etc. I truly wish there was a better phone company in our area, would switch in a heatbeat.

I cannot put into words how terrible T-Mobile has been since I switched my sprint SIM card. “Did you hear T-Mobile bought sprint head over to a store and get your new SIM card and your speeds will be way faster!” They said. They lied. As someone who relies on my data daily for work ever since the switch my interactions with my phone have been nothing but negative, never getting service or being able to do what I need to do even though my phone always says full bars. Complete trash company that’s just screwed  over all its users and customer service is a complete joke on top of it. It’s not like I expect  that much just the slightest bit of functionality of what I pay for. Joke.

They blacklisted my IMEI as "lost or stolen" 
I purchased my phone outright in Canada - I have proof of purchase - I called to ask why it had been blacklisted and they tried to tell me about their deals. I kept saying, I'm in Canada and T-Mobile is not a provider. At least 4 or 5 times.

Customer service rep could barely understand me, I've been on the phone for over 30 minutes now while they work out how to fix this mistake. They asked me to come into a store, again I'M IN CANADA so no I cant just pop down to a store. What a headache

Terrible customer service.  I had Sprint.  Wanted to change plans to T-Mobil Magenta 55+.  Cost $70.00 per month bank draft go 2 phones.  $70.00 includes taxes and fees.  I had to purchase two new phones to get any credits as BOGO is only for new customers.  They gave e me a $400.00 credit as they would not meet AT&T’s new customer and existing customers get the same deal plan.  Phones cost me about $500.00 each. Now my bank draft is $70.00 plus tax. I had to speak with 16 different people to even get the original deal.  People hang up on you.  Some do not speak English very well making it hard to understand at times.  Office of President called me.  Said they would follow up.  First guy never called back when he said he would after I got my first bill to make sure everything was right.  I had several people out of the 26 who said they would call me back and never did.  2nd guy named Brian called me from office of the President.  Said after I used my credit he would call to make sure all was correct.  In mean time I am spending about $79.00 per month for my plan thus using about $9,00 per month up of my credit when monthly rate should be $70.00 per month period.  Some customer reps do not know different plans offered.  I had to tell them what T-Mobil was advertising and they still got it wrongThey say they record conversations but I cannot get anyone to listen to my conversation when ai bought the $70.00 per month Magenta 55+ plan to prove what I purchased. I spent hours on the phone with no real solutions. I could have gone to AT&T and got the BOGO at about the same monthly cost if I have to pay taxes.  Customer service has no way of getting you in touch with previous people one had contact with. I think they know one has no way of getting back with them so they lie to you, hang up on you, tell you what you want to hear, only to learn what they said was it true and you cannot get back to them personally to question them.  Plus it appears no one in authority listens to recordings to get what was actually said.  One last thing, one you get to a customer service rep and they review what was input into the system they very rarely look past the last touch you had with them to get to the root of the issue.  You have tell them to look in depth.  Absolutely the worse customer service.I thought Sprint and Verizon was bad. Apparently they all are bad.  The worse customer service.

I hate it here!!!!! This service is horrible. Customer service is a joke, the phones are a joke, and my bill is a joke. Everything is wrong, service,phone,and the "Experts" so they call themselves, did not resolve my issues and I have lots of them. For the money that I pay, my calls drop, volume is low, I'm just fed up with T mobile.

As a former 20 Year Sprint employee and now independent 4 year Customer with 4 lines, I thought maybe, just maybe Customer Service and Service in general would improve. Instead, it's gotten much worse. I hate to take my business to verizon or ATT or Comcast, but anything has got to be better. I have about 4 hours and multiple trips to Spint and TMobile locations to simply try and switch my Sprint plan to a TMobile Veterans plan (that's right; veterans can't get served with TMobile either), but it seems it's impossible to accomplish. The systems don't work at the stores and when I called TMobile Customer service, after 3o+ minutes on the phone, the # rang in my ear and I was sent to a Spanish speaking Rep whom had to send me back to English Rep whom wanted me to start all over again.

OMG! Is there a Carrier out there that can get it right and just serv the customer? In the most simple of transactions?

My sister came to visit me here in The Villages, Florida last week and she spent most of her time trying to find enough signal to make calls. It seems like you don't care about this area. She called them and they said that she would have to change to another carrier if she was going to stay there because they don't plan on doing anything more in that area for a while. Very cheap poor company ethics.

After 6-years as a customer in their partner group they unceremoniously canceled their contract potentially leaving 5000 international workers, who just lost their jobs due to COVID, without phone service.  I was promised forbearance if I did a deal with Ultra Mobile, their hand-picked champion, who was also a competitor, ‘…and soon’ went the extortion by their director of wholesale. Thankfully I managed to avoid their trap and moved the business elsewhere.  Consider yourself forewarned that they, t-Mobile, are monsters who need heavy handed government regulation.   Much of this happened after their newly acquired dominant position the marketplace after the merger with sprint.  Boycott T-Mobile and their demon offspring ultra Mobile.
Total disasters, this company is a joke, never more .
Spent 15 hours troubleshooting my phone....got so frustrated I walked across the parking lot and switched to verizon....No more problems!!

Nothing works, since Sprint merger.  15 years with Sprint, no issues..now, I can't even complete a phone call, or send text messages, let alone stream music or video.  Nice $1200 paper weight I own now.

This place is a total rip off and scam. My phone was acting up so they sent me to Apple, I pay insurance with Tmobile but they didn't want to help me. Instead I had to pay $99 with apple when t mobile gave me the phone. The sold me a screen protector and claimed that if my phone cracked with the screen protector they would replace it for free. That was a lie instead I had to take it to apple.

Longtime Verizon customer decided to shop.  We chose T-Mobile.  Big mistake.  I have dropped more calls in the 5 weeks we have been with T-Mobile than the past 5 years with Verizon.  I live near Gainesville, GA.  On most of my travels throughout GA there are a number of locations where I cant get Siri to connect to send a text message.

I’ll take some of the blame for not paying attention to the salesman, who was very nice, cordial and helpful.  Bottom line, I’m stuck with inferior service for the next 23 months.  OUCH!

My husband and I were with Sprint for almost 28 years. When we were getting new phones, due to the merger with T-Mobile, we went with T-Mobile. Bad decision.  The sales person in the T-Mobile store said that because we were getting new Samsung phones we could get a Samsung smart watch for FREE! I thought this was a great promotion and accepted it because he also told me the reason my FitBit wouldn't connect with the new phone was because of Samsung's new watches. This was incorrect information, all I had to do was redo my FitBit app to the new phone.  He also didn't tell me that I would be charged for a new telephone line and new item service plan. Once I found that out, I called T-Mobile to return the watch that I hadn't even received yet.  I got the pre-paid label and returned the watch, unopened, the same day I received it. T-Mobile acknowledged receipt of the return.  I figured that was the end of it. But, Oh! No! I get the new bill - I'm still being charged for the phone line and the services for a watch I don't own!  Called customer service today.  The representative (physical location in the Philippines) after a long discussion was going to credit me $15.00 - the incorrect charges were $53.13.  I asked to speak to a supervisor. The rep told me that it might jeopardize her job if she let me speak to a supervisor, she would do it herself.  When I told her the credit should be $53.13, she told me that she only had $50.00 and that she would be paying it out of her own pocket!  T-Mobile is a huge company and while I realize that customer service is outsourced, having an employee pay with their own funds is outrageous for any company as large as T-Mobile.  Was she lying? Who knows but it is an unacceptable way to do business.  Period.  After more requests to speak to a supervisor and being denied, the rep finally said the after she spoke to the supervisor, I would be credited $53.00 immediately. Still waiting for that.

Based on my experience, I would not recommend T-Mobile due to subtle deceit from in-person sales people and/or customer service telephone reps.

I want to leave a review so other people can see it. While the representative in the actual store failed me, the representatives in the call center in Tampa have gone above and beyond to help me. At one point I even had to speak to a supervisor, and he was able to fix the situation fairly quickly.
I see a lot of negative feedback on this page, and I want others to know that if they are able to fix a situation that they absolutely do.

A few weeks ago we took advantage of a promotion on the Galaxy S21 plus phones. They were buy one get one! The lady at the store told us that we could save money by switching are phone plans, and just getting a hotspot for the house... letting me lose a wonderful plan I was grandfathered into. The plan I was originally on is unlimited hotspot through my phone for $25 a month. Instead I was  talked into paying $55 for a hundred gigs a month... yeah that was not fair. Our bill ended up being well over what our original bill was, even before paying on the new device.
The representative I spoke to on the phone did the best they could to fix what they could. However I still managed to have to talk to her supervisor, so that he could switch his back to our original plans.
Flash Forward to today, I got my new bill update... $150 more than what my original bill is usually. So of course I was angry. I knew I would be paying more since I was paying on a device, and the fact that we have protection plans in place on both of our phones. But $150 was not the right amount...
So again I called back, I wanted to speak to the supervisor I talk to you before just because he was familiar with my information prior. He was on another call at the time, so the representative I spoke to spoke it over with his supervisor, and was able to come up with a solution. He solved the problem and Save the Day! 
My situation was very confusing, it was even confusing for me to have to explain to them. But each representative and supervisor understood and fixed the issue. 

As I said, the girl inside of the store was terrible. She lied to us up front and cause a whole lot of issues... but thank God for those supervisors at the call center.

I called to take advantage of a promo. The rep said he would give me 800 bucks for my s10 plus and all I pay is the difference between the 800 bucks and the i phone 12  pro max cost...
 I got my i phone and ups'd the s10 to the address on the tag they sent me. After 12 plus calls over a 3 week period of a sea of calls they were clueless..

   I had the tracking # which showed received. They said they could not find it. I was told I would get a call when they did find it and i never heard a peep. I did get a hold one rep, after seeing my bill went from 50.00 to 90 bucks. This rep and I went round and round for the 12th time, 12th call etc..So, , she got her mgr, of course..

  He offered me market value for my s10 plus. 170 bucks and took that off the price of the i phone.. 
...SOO, I paid 200 bucks, I sent them my phone and I am in the end paying full price for this apple. Which I could bought on my own...
..Mind you this 3 week F*$# fest was all Documented via text and via phone calls.. I was told one thing, got sucked in and left with a phone bill that has doubled. gone is my s10 plus and 200 bucks. What an absolute nightmare.. When the phone is paid for, I am going to drop them and go to One of the other big ones..
 They dont even deserve my 50.00 a month.

The worst company on the planet!! Can't believe the BS!! My son, an active duty sailor, was going to switch to T-mobile for the military discount. So, we all decided to switch with him. They stated no charge to switch but charged him $140 for the SIM cards! SIM cards they couldn't even get working in our phones!! The store referred us to call in but the phones were not working. Thought at first it was a joke... We canceled that day. A real hassle to get the $140 refund, but managed after the fourth call and hours of our time. Now they are chasing my son for the $44 of charges for the mobile plan!! Seriously! HATE HATE HATE this company.

Different amounts charged every month and I have to call and explain it over and over to get them to fix their errors. T moblile is the worst. I have only been with t mobile for two months, all I had was no signal, dropped calls at home. The internet dropped as well, even with a $300.00 signal booster. That didn't end there, I was also charged for two lines even though l only wanted one! I told the customer service multiple times and told them I only wanted one line, they kept saying we will fix that and never did. I also wanted to get my coverage in my area to be better, and they said that a cell tower was right down the street from me and they can't under stand why my phone keeps dropping calls. They said that I needed a new phone, and I told them it is a new phone and I am still paying payments on that one. Then they told me that all I have to do is trade in the one I have for a newer one that cost more money than the first one. I told them that I can't afford that new one they where talking about. I am on a fixed income, then they said we could knock down the service agreement to try to save me money. That didn't work any better. So I got fed up with there BS, and went back to verizon. They said that I still owe money on the new phone, and I told them that I will gladly give it back. They said that they can't take it back. So I said I will call the BBB, which I did. The BBB contacted t mobile and informed them I will never go bact to t mobile, and until this day I never went back. I am with and will stay with verizon! 

Warning to all who read: never go to t mobile as they will rip you off, and get your money. The worst company ever! 

Suggestion: go to verizon, or even AT&T for phone service.

1 Honestly you deserve no stores because 🖕🏽you and your company 2 for last two months I’ve been nothing but patient because you guys tell me excuses for why my service is bad I can’t even get a single bar at my main job site I have to go searching around for signal bar or tonight I had to drive around three to locations looking for a signal so I decide to go to T-Mobile store which I’m at right now and guess what they lock the Wi-Fi night honestly that’s the worst f’ing company ever and I can’t wait to go back to cricket these pound for pound they beat the 💩out of your company specially on services in signals if you gonna have a dog s💩 service you better charge of dog 💩 price not give us all stuck up f’ing💩 signals  so anyone who thinks about switching over please dont

Had a 24 month agreement with T-mobile. It was a nightmare! Beginning with them losing my 4 trade ins, sending them to two different states. I had to prove that they existed and it took them a week or so to track them down. My family had numerous issues with their devices and I several business calls and texts dropped lost or just never came through to me. I got tired of calling their customer service for issues because, for one, most of them are foreigners and I couldn’t understand them and they had a hard time understanding my issues, so the language barrier is a huge negative. Finally, when my 24 month sentence was served they tried to charge me an extra 3 months for my devices, when I called them on it, they agreed with me, confirmed I was correct, and told me they corrected the issue. I paid my final bill and went back to Verizon where I receive superb service and reception. Two weeks later I get a bill from T-Mobile saying I owe another month on my devices. Needless to say my final phone call to them wasn’t pleasant, nor did they bother to correct the issue. Again some foreign rep had an issue understanding my explanation. I was on the phone for 41 minutes and the last 15 I was on hold, so I hung up. T-Mobile is the worst carrier I’ve experienced and I’ve had Sprint as well. I put this out there so I can save someone the misery my family and I went through. DO NOT GIVE YOUR MONEY TO T-MOBILE.

2nd after standing in the store for n hr they sent me to a different store a few miles up the road thinking one of their guys could fix it  stood in there another hr still no success  so they sent a ticket to tech support and the in store guy offered a credit on my  bill  for standing in both stores and said he would follow up on Monday  he did send me a text asking if it was resolved on Monday i said no it wasn't  and that was the last Ive heard in 2 months. no more follow up and no credit to my bill

3rd i truly understand now the saying  u get what u pay for  t mobile is cheaper but the service is garbage i have had so many dropped calls since ive switched  i mean ive had so many im now in the habit of saying  r u there in the middle of the conversation to make sure its not a another dropped call

If I could give zero stars I would. Sprint was good until they merged with T-Mobile, once they merged it was torture. Lost signal in most areas, was deceived numerous of times by this company. Customer service on the phone was very rude, and unprofessional. Overall I was unsatisfied with this company and wouldn’t recommend it to anyone. They are a complete joke.

Their service is horrible and they lie and mislead the customers. They have different policies for different customers. They don't have uniformed polices for each customers. Their beyond rude. They charged disconnection fees even doing covid19

(Monday)Placed order for Hotspot to be shipped overnight to the store. (Tuesday) called did not arrive, was asked if I signed an E-document. I told them I never received the document. Was told UPS had not shown up yet but would call me when UPS came in, Never heard anything. (Wednesday) called, still hadn't shown up, t-mobile employee told me he would call customer service, he called me back and told me they told him that it would arrive that day. He told me he would check UPS and call me, Never heard anything. asked if manager was in, was told he would be out until Thursday. (Thursday) Today, called, still had not shown up, finally spoke with Manager, got nothing but excuses. he asked me again if i had signed the Document, I told him that I had informed his employee on Tuesday that I never received the document. He went on to tell me he was a new employee, (the employee had told me he had been working there for 3 weeks) I ask the manger why was he having someone answer the phone if he didn't know what he was talking about, and why did he lie to me if that was the case. The manager then became very apathetic about the situation which only made me angrier. after the call I tried to get  a regional managers phone number but only got customer service. the whole point in me going to the store was so I didn't have to sit on the phone. I talked to someone at customer service which basically told me the same thing, so I asked if they sent me the document, would I have the device by tomorrow (Friday) as I was leaving for a trip, the reason for ordering the device to begin with. they could not tell me and as usual of late, English was not the person's primary language. I give her credit for her efforts as this situation was not her fault it was the store's. I proceeded to ask for a supervisor, and I am awaiting a return call that was supposed to be 5 min after I spoke with her, as of now, almost 30 min has gone by without a call.

BUYER BEWARE!  I've had a family plan on Sprint for 20 years without any issues.  Since the merger with T-Mobile I was billed for 4 months for a new line that I never ordered, they finally canceled the new line and credited my account.  During the same call I was offered a promotion to upgrade two phones on my account which I accepted.  I was then transferred to "tele-sales" spent over an hour placing the order however the called dropped before the order was finalized, when I called back the next day to check the status of the order there was no record of it. I relayed the promotion I was offered only to be told that the promotion I was offered was now expired, after pleading my case to a retention specialist they agreed to honor the promotion.  I was then transferred to "tele-sales" to finalize the order which I spent another hour and a half doing however before finalizing the order and after now nearly two hours later the call mysteriously disconnected.  They always promise to call back in the event the call is disconnected but this promise is only lip service.  It's now 2 weeks later and I still have not been able to finalize my order.  This company is absolutely unethical with no regard for their consumers.  RUN. Always get an "Interaction ID #" so that you have a record of your call.  They outsourced sales and it's a complete disaster.

We were happy Sprint customers for many years, with 5 phones on our plan and excellent coverage. With our forced change to TMobile, we now have very spotty coverage. Phone calls are dropped and conversations break up. After 5 phone calls to TMobile, I was told two Sprint towers near us shut down and we must now connect to the TMobile Towers. So disappointed. Researching a new carrier. RIP Sprint.
On vacation dealing with t-mobile service issues. If you’re traveling get ready to focus on t-mobiles service rather than having fun. T-mobiles T stands for trash. Avoid!!.

T-Moibile service is very unprofessional!! On 3/12/21 I went to the T-Mobile store managed by Ivan Martinez inside the Millenia Mall in Orlando, Florida. I was interested in getting a new iPhone 12 Pro with T-Mobile on their Magenta plan. I was approved to make 36 monthly payments for the iPhone 12 Pro, apparently under the Jump On Demand program. On 3/14/21, I returned to the store with my wife Aixa Figueroa, to get her on my plan. The manager of the store offered us the buy-one get-one-free iPhone deal. They informed us that since I bought my iPhone 3 days, we would qualify for that deal. We left that day content with two new iPhone 12 Pro’s. However, the next day my wife noticed that her iPhone 12 Pro with T-Mobile was not receiving any calls or texts. Instead, all her phone calls and text messages were going to her old iPhone Xs Max with Verizon. We went back to the store at Millenia Mall on 3/15/21 to inquire about this problem. They stated that it could take as long as 72 hours for a number to transfer and for us to remain patient. As they accessed my account, they also realized that they made a mistake because we were not supposed to get the buy-one, get-one deal when the first phone was bought on Jump on Demand. In order to correct my account, they made us pay $50 additional dollars to satisfy the down payment amount for the Equipment Installment Plan (EIP), and they also changed our Magenta plan to the Magenta Plus plan, which we did request.  We were unaware of the change to Magenta Plus until we returned to the store two days later on 3/17/21, because my wife’s iPhone hadn’t activated with her phone number yet. By 3/19/21, my wife’s phone still was not activated with T-Mobile, after Verizon already released the number and the plan was still Magenta Plus. Our problems failed to be resolved, therefore we returned on 3/19/21 to the T-Mobile store at the Millenia Mall to express our dissatisfaction to management and to request a complete refund on the items bought. The store manager agreed to return the entire amount we spent on the devices without charging a restocking fee. Before leaving the store, we made sure to remind them to close our account because we did not want any recurring fee on our checking account. They assured us the account was terminated and that we would not receive any further fees. They told us to not worry about anything else.
On 5/1/21, I receive a charge of $224.25 on my account, making me overdraft significantly on my account. That same day, I called T-Mobile to inquire as to why I was charged if my account was closed on 3/19/21 and I no longer had the T-Mobile iPhones. They continued to explain that they were not going to refund me my money because this was a pro-rated charge that I supposedly owed for the month of April 2021. This was impossible because I closed my account on 3/19/21 and was no longer using their services. I demanded to speak to a manager, and eventually they agreed to return the $224.25 to my account INCLUDING the $35 overdraft fee my bank charged me. On 5/1/21, I received a refund for $35 from T-Mobile but I never received the rest of the $224.25 that they promised to refund me. I called and spoke to T-Mobile customer service about six additional times, but they refuse to help me!

T-Mobile has taken much needed money from me for an account that I closed on 3/19/21 and now refuses to refund me money that I rightfully own. Every time we call and get ahold of a T-Mobile manager on the phone in attempt to get the problem resolved, our phone call magically fails. I am the only person who works in my household due to the COVID-19 pandemic and this situation has paced my family in a very unpleasant financial situation. I would like T-Mobile to return my $224.25 that they took from me for an account that THEY failed to close on 3/19/21 when we requested it of them.

Being forced to switch from Sprint to T Moblie. Is the worst thing to happen. Sprint was fine on its own and it actually had better service and better coverage. Why? They had more towers I belive already set up. And t moblie barely has any let alone there are barely 5g towers. Even the internet with t mobile. I have a cell tower literally in my back yard, my Download speeds are nearly 600mbs but guess what even with that, calls are dropped, texts don't send. Internet pages don't load, playing online games I'm randomly disconnected from the internet. I left Cox for T mobile not only for the amazing price they have, and being next to a cell tower to have better internet that Cox and it's starting to become WORSE than Cox. At least with paying nearly $200 for Cox I was still able to stay connected online for online games and have very very little wifi issues.

It's been a month since I signed with T Mobile and if I could, I'd leave.  When signing up, agent said she checked and there is excellent coverage where I live. There isn't!! My wife drops calls all the time.  Yesterday, a business call was dropped no less than six times.  When I signed up, I was told they have signal ampifiers if that is ever a problem.  IT IS!!  Rarely had the problem with Verizon.  I called to get an amplifier.  I have to pay for their weak signal that they told me was excellent?? I ordered and paid a couple weeks ago, then was told they don't have any.  Still waiting.  Called to check on order and recorded message says go online.  I go online and get message "if you ordered within 48 hours, wait a little longer"......like weeks?? Tracking online is a ridiculous circle jerk bringing you right back to "check order status" and the 48 hour message.  Still dropping calls.....
